assertions.text: Imports and uses DataflowEngine from rete-engine with editor.use(engine)
assertions.evidence: Line: `import { DataflowEngine } from 'rete-engine'` and `editor.use(engine)` present. Engine is created and wired before nodes are added.

assertions.text: data() method on AddNode reads inputs with double-bracket array access pattern (e.g. inputs.left?.[0]?.[0]) rather than single-bracket
assertions.evidence: `const left = inputs.left?.[0]?.[0] ?? 0; const right = inputs.right?.[0]?.[0] ?? 0;` — correctly uses double-bracket access matching DataflowEngine's array-of-arrays output shape.

assertions.text: ReactPlugin is initialized with { createRoot } argument (required for React 19)
assertions.evidence: `const render = new ReactPlugin<Schemes, AreaExtra>({ createRoot });` — createRoot is imported and passed.

assertions.text: Plugin wiring follows correct order: editor.use(area), area.use(connection), area.use(render)
assertions.evidence: Order is: editor.use(area) → area.use(connection) → area.use(render) → editor.use(engine). Matches required four-layer order.

assertions.text: engine.reset() is called before engine.fetch() to clear cached results
assertions.evidence: `engine.reset(); return (await engine.fetch(add.id))` — reset is called inside the compute() helper.
